title: Workflow Audit Checklist for Service Businesses
subtitle: Find the broken processes bleeding time and money from your $1M-$20M service business in 60-90 minutes

sections.title: Client Intake & Onboarding
sections.description: This is where most service businesses start losing time before they even deliver anything. Every manual step here multiplies across every new client.

items.text: Capture all new client information in one system instead of scattering it across email, forms, and Slack
items.tip: Pick your CRM or project tool as the single source and route every intake form there via webhook or native integration.

items.text: Template client onboarding tasks and assign them automatically when a deal closes
items.tip: Build one master onboarding template per service tier, then trigger it from your CRM stage change.

items.text: Send welcome emails and kickoff materials without someone manually hitting send
items.tip: Use a sequence tool tied to the deal-won event so nothing depends on memory.

items.text: Provision client access to portals, folders, and tools without opening an IT ticket
items.tip: Automate folder creation and permissions via Google Drive, Notion, or SharePoint APIs on the onboarding trigger.

sections.title: Project & Job Management
sections.description: If your team is copy-pasting job details between systems or chasing down status updates, you are paying for coordination instead of execution.

items.text: Trigger tasks across all relevant systems automatically the moment a job is created
items.tip: Map every downstream system once, then fan out from a single job-created event so nothing is entered twice.

items.text: Route status updates into one place instead of siloing them in email threads or DMs
items.tip: Designate one project tool as canonical and push Slack or email updates into it via integration, not the other way around.

items.text: Log handoffs between team members so they are visible rather than verbal or assumed
items.tip: Require a status change plus owner reassignment in your project tool to count as a real handoff.

items.text: Maintain project templates for repeatable work so nothing gets rebuilt from scratch
items.tip: Audit your last 10 projects, find the 3 most common shapes, and template those first.

sections.title: Communication & Notifications
sections.description: Most communication problems are actually workflow problems. If people need to ask for updates, the system is not doing its job.

items.text: Send clients automated updates at key milestones instead of waiting for someone to remember
items.tip: Define 4-6 milestone events per engagement and wire each to a templated client-facing message.

items.text: Trigger internal team notifications based on workflow events rather than manual pings
items.tip: Replace recurring check-in pings with Slack alerts fired by status changes or SLA timers.

items.text: Maintain a single source of truth for client communication history across email, calls, and chat
items.tip: Pipe every channel into your CRM contact record so nobody has to ask where the last conversation lives.

items.text: Route escalations automatically when deadlines are missed or tasks stall beyond threshold
items.tip: Set stall timers on critical task statuses and auto-assign to a manager when breached.

sections.title: Billing & Invoicing
sections.description: Companies routinely leave 10-15% of revenue on the table because invoicing is manual and inconsistent. This section finds that leak.

items.text: Generate invoices automatically from project milestones, contracts, or logged time
items.tip: Connect your project tool to QuickBooks or Stripe so billable events create draft invoices without human entry.

items.text: Reconcile logged hours and expenses against invoices before they go out
items.tip: Run a weekly variance report comparing time tracked to time billed and flag gaps over 5%.

items.text: Send payment reminders and collect overdue balances on an automated cadence
items.tip: Set a 3-touch dunning sequence at 7, 14, and 30 days past due before any human gets involved.

items.text: Track every recurring client on an auto-renewing contract or subscription
items.tip: Move month-to-month clients onto Stripe subscriptions or signed auto-renew terms to stop quiet revenue leaks.

sections.title: Reporting & Visibility
sections.description: If you cannot see where work is stuck, you cannot fix it. This section exposes the blind spots that let bottlenecks compound quietly.

items.text: Review a live dashboard showing project status, capacity, and margin without asking anyone
items.tip: Pull project, time, and invoice data into one dashboard in Metabase, Looker Studio, or Notion.

items.text: Flag at-risk projects automatically based on budget burn, deadline slip, or stalled tasks
items.tip: Define 2-3 risk signals per project type and surface them on the dashboard in red.

completion_cta.headline: You Found the Leaks. Now Fix the Biggest One First.
completion_cta.description: Count your unchecked boxes, especially the high-impact ones. Pick the single workflow costing you the most hours or dollars per week and automate that one before touching anything else. One fix at a time compounds faster than ten half-built systems.
